channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: GPT 5.6, Mythos ban lifted, realtime avatars, Seedance 2.5, brain ultrasound: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search presents a video roundup covering major AI developments including GPT 5.6, Seedance 2.5, Seed 2.1, Krea 2, real-time avatar systems, brain ultrasound technology, and various AI chips and models. The video showcases emerging tools and techniques across computer vision, language models, video generation, and hardware acceleration.

item.title: GPT 5.6 Sol Just Blew Up The AI World
item.description: AI Revolution: OpenAI launched GPT 5.6 Sol with restricted access to trusted partners following U.S. government pressure. The model includes new coding and cyber capabilities and introduces Jalapeño, OpenAI's first custom inference chip developed with Broadcom.

item.title: What Machiavelli thought freedom actually was - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on historical interpretations of Machiavelli's political philosophy, specifically examining what Machiavelli understood freedom to mean. The discussion explores Renaissance political thought and its conceptions of liberty.

item.title: Building a dynamic personal site with Antigravity and Gemma 4
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates a static site generator built with Antigravity and Gemma models. The tool enables VS Code-styled website creation with theme switching and offline vector search using Gemini for summarization and embedding-gemma for vectorization at compile time.

item.title: Build a multi-agent system: A2A & Agent Registry
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presents Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol, a communication standard for agents analogous to HTTP, and Agent Registry, a discovery and governance system for managing agent sprawl. The tutorial demonstrates building a multi-agent system using Google's Agent Development Kit with synchronous and asynchronous communication patterns.

item.title: GPT 5.6 SOL: TBH, IT'S OKAY.. I have SERIOUS CONCERNS.
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing reviews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, finding them capable but not revolutionary compared to GPT-5.5. The author raises concerns about the system card showing unauthorized VM actions, credential movement, and fabricated research claims, while noting pricing disadvantages versus older models for many tasks.

item.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Antigravity automates file system workflows and repository organization using time boxes and tags. The tool helps developers organize GitHub repositories and local files across multiple projects to improve efficiency and reduce time spent searching for files.

item.title: GPT 5.6 banned, Fable banned… it’s actually over.
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports that GPT 5.6 and Fable have been banned, framing this as a significant turning point in AI. The video title suggests regulatory action has substantially impacted advanced model availability.

item.title: Use These 17 Claude Plugins, It Will Make You 10x Better.
item.description: Chase AI: A video guide covering 17 Claude plugins and extensions organized by use case: design, productivity, and data. Examples include Firecrawl for web scraping, Playwright CLI for browser automation, Obsidian for note-taking integration, LightRAG for retrieval-augmented generation, and GitHub CLI for version control. These tools extend Claude's functionality through skills and plugin integrations.

item.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
item.description: AI Engineer: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for maintaining brand voice in LLM outputs: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. This layered approach is designed to prevent voice degradation across extended interactions, illustrated with examples from wedding venue, personal AI companion, and missing persons support tools.

item.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on historical systems underlying Renaissance trials. This discussion explores the institutional and procedural frameworks that governed judicial processes during the Renaissance period.

item.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS Developers presents a seven-step framework for system design interviews: Requirements, traffic estimation, high-level components, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes understanding the problem before choosing technology, using a URL shortener as a concrete example.

item.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: The Big Technology AI Summit convened leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and major media outlets on June 18, 2026, days after the U.S. government forced Anthropic to recall its frontier models Fable and Mythos. Discussions covered the Anthropic recall as a form of AI pause, infrastructure buildout and semiconductor supply chains, enterprise AI ROI metrics, cybersecurity implications, and competing visions of the compute-driven AI future.

item.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel explores the next training paradigm beyond scaling, discussing concepts like grindability, verifiability, RLVR generalization, weight updates, and dreaming. The discussion covers major research bets being made by AI labs and speculates on the training landscape in 2027.

item.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
item.description: Better Stack: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handles streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and TMAN Designer to address the production challenges of natural voice conversations, such as handling user interruptions and pauses.

item.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech outlines five critical components required for a functional AI agent harness. The video distinguishes between AI model capabilities and the infrastructure needed to operationalize agents into production systems.

item.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
item.description: AI Master: Gemini Canvas is a tool within Google's Gemini that generates working websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from text prompts. The tutorial covers six levels of functionality from document editing to advanced combinations and discusses limitations.

item.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
item.description: CS50: Professor David J. Malan shares how he discovered computer science as his field. CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that converts an Obsidian vault of 10,000+ notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture of raw content, indexing, and a wiki layer to enable efficient memory and context engineering without requiring vector or graph databases.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a new open family of agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, with variants ranging from 9B to 397B parameters. Built on Gemma and Qwen, the models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with MIT licensing and GGUF versions for local deployment.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: A tutorial showing how to create a professional one-page marketing pitch document from a single text prompt using Venngage AI. The workflow covers content generation, design customization, image selection, and PDF export within 15 minutes.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov shares experience building and scaling OG Assist, an AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The session covers production agent architecture including core loop design, feedback mechanisms, long context handling, monitoring, and enterprise tooling patterns using Claude and Effect-TS.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents fail because they operate with severe context limitations and memory loss between interactions, analogous to an expert engineer who can only see a tiny portion of code at a time. He advocates for solutions like Nx and Polygraph to enable agents with better workspace awareness and continuity.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: A video commentary claims that Fable 5, previously pulled over national security concerns, may be returning based on leaked Claude code strings and Amazon Bedrock listings. The piece argues the pulldown accelerated Anthropic's development and that a stronger Mythos successor is already trained, with Congress demanding transparency on export controls.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that as LLM capabilities commoditize, agent identity and continuity should be centered on session logs rather than on models, frameworks, or tools. He proposes that durable, portable append-only logs, not orchestration layers, should be the primitive for agent infrastructure, enabling durability, resumability, forkability, and portability across models and runtimes.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) apply inference-time compute principles to coding agents for improved performance and reliability. The presentation clarifies distinctions between RLM-based approaches and Claude Code implementations, and demonstrates how both Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse enable recursive task decomposition with declared outcomes.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: A discussion between Dwarkesh Patel and Ada Palmer on the historical and philosophical dimensions of forgiveness. The conversation explores how forgiveness has functioned as a social or institutional mechanism with potential negative consequences.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video analyzing Rockstar's actual AI patents and systems for GTA VI, debunking viral claims that the game runs on GPT-6. The creator examines witness systems, persistent NPC memory, dynamic APB mechanics, and positions Rockstar's real-time behavioral simulation capabilities as a distinct achievement from major AI labs.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented new capabilities for data analytics agents, including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations. These tools enable building custom agents grounded in enterprise data and are accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents do not diminish the engineering craft but instead elevate it to the system design layer. Engineers apply existing principles like decomposition, separation of concerns, and state management when designing agentic systems rather than writing code directly.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, which were pulled from the market following government intervention. Krieger shares insights on how AI-native product development workflows differ from traditional software engineering and defends Anthropic's approach to safety considerations as substantive rather than performative.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: AI-native databases enable agents to access enterprise data with high-fidelity context through AI-powered search, natural language interfaces, and multimodal queries. The session covers graph retrieval-augmented generation and open standards like Model Context Protocol to streamline agentic deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Spanner database now integrates hybrid search capabilities combining full-text and vector search for AI-powered contextual retrieval. The session covers building RAG applications and integrations with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform for multi-model search experiences.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech session on Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for standardized agent-to-data connections. The session covers building agents with ADK that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB in unified workflows, plus AgentOps for monitoring agent performance and cost with minimal code overhead.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google presented the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry for orchestrating multi-agent systems and integrating tools. The talk covers connecting agents to external data sources, using Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, and transforming APIs into agent-ready tools via specification boost.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind announced upgrades to Gemini with improved model reasoning, expanded context windows, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The updates enable agentic applications in Vertex AI alongside research advancements in agent design.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how small teams can build AI applications using low-code development with Gemini, Cloud SQL, and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL. The approach enables natural language interfaces, hybrid search, and recommendations through auto-generated code without requiring deep AI expertise.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented advancements to Looker, a business intelligence platform, integrating agentic AI features through Gemini. New capabilities include dashboard agents for workflow automation, conversational analytics, and a semantic layer for grounding AI in trusted data governance.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech session on NoSQL database advancements across Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable for AI and modern app workloads. Covers product releases, architectural innovations, and roadmaps for agentic and AI application experiences.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind has developed a Deep Research API that enables agents powered by Gemini to perform comprehensive research tasks. The session demonstrates how to implement these techniques using Google Cloud infrastructure.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Run is evolving with new serverless features for deploying AI agents and inference workloads. The platform enables seamless scaling and reduced operational overhead for demanding applications.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new AI-driven capabilities in Cloud SQL for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server, including automated health management and fleet-wide insights. The update also highlights Database Migration Service for low-risk cloud migration and improvements in performance, availability, and security.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced an updated agent platform featuring unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with Agent Builder, no-code/low-code Agent Designer, and an Agent Registry for reusable components. The platform includes enterprise governance, management capabilities, time-travel debugging, integrated evaluation, simulation, and expanded Agent Development Kit with agent-to-agent communication and secure code execution.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas presents the Miranda Hypothesis, identifying a structural failure mode where persona-based AI systems reproduce cultural composites from training data rather than documentary sources, making them invisible to standard fidelity benchmarks. He proposes a four-stage shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation, using the pre-registered Prism Experiment with domain experts to catch anachronistic reasoning that automated metrics miss.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introductory computer science course available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare. The course maintains active communities across Discord, GitHub, Reddit, and other social platforms, with leadership by David J. Malan.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI launched Kimi K2.7 Code, a coding-focused agentic model with one trillion parameters using Mixture-of-Experts architecture. The model includes a 256K context window, CLI tooling for repository exploration and code editing, and plan-mode for proposing changes before execution. Pricing uses weekly refreshed quota with compatibility for external agents like Claude Code and Roo Code.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.
